comments.summary: Squared 3D vector-length helper: computes v[0]^2 + v[1]^2 + v[2]^2 from one float[3] pointer.

inferred: Parameter kept as float * to match existing kb.json declaration; access pattern is read-only and could also be represented as const float *.

comments.summary: Squared 3D distance helper: computes sum of squared component deltas between two float[3] vectors.

inferred: Parameter typing narrowed to const float * from direct [arg+0/4/8] float loads and ai_clump callsite passing actor/record position vectors at +0xbc.
The double constant at 0x2533d0 (~0.0001) serves as a minimum magnitude threshold for normalization; vectors shorter than this are treated as zero-length.
Object name vector_math.obj is provisional; the true .obj name is unknown since the function has no string references or assert paths.

comments.summary: Initializes two AI trigonometric lookup table regions (7-float records) from static angle/scale constant tables.

inferred: Table element semantics are inferred from math pattern and write layout; symbolic names for backing globals/constants remain unknown.

0x3aa90.name: actors_dispose_from_old_map
inferred: FUN_0x3aa50 is actors_dispose, the single-function mirror of the dispose-pair convention seen elsewhere in this subsystem.
FUN_0x3aa50 is actors_dispose, the single-function mirror of the dispose-pair convention seen elsewhere in this subsystem.
uncertain: Whether actors.obj contains additional unported functions such as actors_initialize_for_new_map at 0x3a990 has not been confirmed.
Whether actors.obj contains additional unported functions such as actors_initialize_for_new_map at 0x3a990 has not been confirmed.

inferred: local_14 in the decompiler output is iter.field_0x14 of the extended AI actor iterator; it holds the current actor handle saved by FUN_00059b50.
uncertain: 0x5ab270 (extended actor iterator data) and 0x5ab23c (clump data_t) are not yet in kb.json; used via raw pointer access.

inferred: All 5 csmemset calls and 8 init helpers confirmed from disasm; ADD ESP,0x3c at 0x41172 accounts for all 15 pushed args.
globals[0] = first-frame flag, globals[1] = AI active, globals[2] = pending-spawn/scripted flag; semantics inferred from branch structure.
0x5ab23c is likely a swarm/clump data_t pointer; used as the data source for data_iterator_new.
iter.datum_handle (at EBP-0xc, overlapping the standard data_iter_t) is used in the squad check at 0x424f0 as the decompiler's 'local_10' variable.
uncertain: Float constants 0x2533d8, 0x254cc0, 0x254cc8, 0x254e74 are addresses of game-binary float data; their semantic meaning (range thresholds) is inferred from context only.

inferred: XNet startup params struct at EBP-0xC is 11 bytes matching XNET_STARTUP_PARAMS: cfgSizeOfStruct=0xB, cfgFlags, cfgSockMaxDgramSockets=24, cfgSockMaxStreamSockets=8, cfgSockDefaultRecvBufsizeInK=4, cfgSockDefaultSendBufsizeInK=8, cfgKeyRegMax=128.
uncertain: The function at 0x222ecf (stdcall 1 arg) is likely XNetGetEthernetLinkStatus or a wrapper polling network readiness; exact XNet API name unconfirmed.
The function at 0x222e0e (stdcall 2 args, passes a buffer and size 8) is likely setsockopt or XNetSetSystemLinkPort; exact identity unconfirmed.

inferred: xnet_send thunk at 0x225c20 uses __stdcall convention with RET 0x10 (4 dwords) confirmed from 0x2258a4 epilogue; ep flags byte at offset 4 (bit 0=connected) and ep status int16_t at offset 6 derived from MOV/AND disassembly.
Switch jump table at 0x83010 with byte-redirect table at 0x8301c maps WSAECONNRESET (0x2733) to -4, abort-class errors (0x2744/0x2745/0x2746/0x2749/0x274a/0x274c) to -3, all others to -2.
winsock_error_report at 0x83310 is cdecl with 1 int arg and void return, based on PUSH EAX / CALL / ADD ESP,4 pattern and no use of return value.
xnet_closesocket at 0x225cc6 is stdcall with 1 int arg returning int, based on PUSH EAX / CALL and callee RET 4.

destroy_endpoint calls close_endpoint (0x84000), then debug_free with original XBE source path/line (0xe8), then tail-calls endpoint_pool_cleanup (0x82d30); all three are internal to transport_endpoint_winsock.c TU.
uncertain: close_endpoint (0x84000) and endpoint_pool_cleanup (0x82d30) are grouped under transport_endpoint_set_winsock.obj in kb.json for convenience, but their XBE assert strings reference transport_endpoint_winsock.c (line 0x221/0x222 for close_endpoint), suggesting they may belong to a separate unrecovered transport_endpoint_winsock.obj.

comments.summary: Per-tick update for all local-player observers: records delta_time, validates header/trailer signatures and updated_for_frame, then dispatches three unported sub-updates (0x8b060 input, 0x8cd40 integration when dt changed, 0x8c4b0 post) via inline asm passing index in EAX.
Asserting strcat helper: validates destination/source pointers, scans to destination NUL, then appends source including terminator using dword-copy plus tail-byte loops.

Advance all active recorded animation threads by one tick: tick per-type event stream via vtable, sanity-check against debug state, apply frame to unit; finished threads restore unit flags and are deleted.

inferred: Error-once flags at 0x4557de (locked) and 0x4557df (permanent) are function-static-style globals not in kb.json; accessed via hardcoded addresses.

comments.summary: Clears permanent decal state for a cluster across 5 decal layers: walks per-layer linked lists, validates cluster ownership, unsets permanent flag, decrements permanent_count, and releases matching decals.

Decal placement gate wrapper: optionally seeds local random from origin, pushes collision-user depth, traces with FUN_0014df70, conditionally emits decal via FUN_0009ac90, then restores collision depth and local seed.
Returns whether two teams are friendly by checking a 10x10 bitfield at game_allegiance_globals+0xa4
Sets allegiance friendship between two teams, updates incidents and hostility bitfields symmetrically, calls game_allegiance_apply_change

inferred: The +0x94 bitfield tracks allegiance incidents (set=incident occurred, clear=no incident).
Function at 0x40010 (game_allegiance_apply_change) propagates allegiance changes to AI encounter state.
game_allegiance_set uses register args in the original: EAX=entry, BL=friendship, stack param=force. All callers are within game_allegiance.obj so no cross-object thunk is needed.
uncertain: The name game_allegiance_apply_change for 0x40010 is inferred from its behavior (logging allegiance broken/reformed and updating AI encounters); the original name is unknown.

comments.summary: Creates or reuses an allegiance entry between two teams, populates fields and sets to hostile
Removes allegiance entry between two teams, sets to friendly and swaps with last entry
Bumps incident count on allegiance entry, flips to friendly if threshold reached

inferred: Tail-call to vtable slot 0x58/4=22; original uses JMP ECX after POP EBP so the vtable entry executes as if it were the original function.
Type mask 4 passed to object_try_and_get_and_verify_type; the function is named unit_can_enter_seat but the object type is a weapon/flag, not a vehicle seat.
uncertain: The disassembly shows param_2 (seat_object_handle) is passed to FUN_000fb0c0 (weapon_is_flag) which checks for CTF/oddball flags, not vehicle seats. The seat semantics may differ from what the name implies.

inferred: Bitmask at offset 0x20 is 0x23 (bits 0,1,5), unlike all other variants which use 3 (bits 0,1 only).
Original binary does not zero the local buffer before setting fields; relies on caller pre-zeroing the output or individual field stores covering all relevant bytes.
Iron CTF uses 2.0f speed (0x40000000) instead of the standard 1.0f (0x3f800000) at offset 0x3C.
The engine-type table at 0x2eff98 is a vtable-pointer array indexed by variant->engine_type (variant+0x18). Exactly 0x1a dwords (0x68 bytes) are copied via MOVSD.REP matching the game_variant_t size.

Phase-2 timer comparison against 0x2533c0 (0.0f): the original uses FCOMP + FNSTSW with bits 0x41 — this is a <= 0 test, reproduced faithfully. Phase-3 polls game_engine_check_input_button with four EDI values in the order seen in disassembly.
uncertain: The game_engine_check_input_button (@edi) forward thunk only works as a raw function pointer; if the function is ever ported to C the inline asm in game_engine_update_non_deterministic must be revisited.

inferred: String addresses in disassembly (0x26c73c=race, 0x26c730=team_race, etc.) verified against XBE .rdata section via manual VA-to-file-offset mapping; Ghidra flat-read of the file showed wrong values but the XBE section table confirms race/team_race etc.
game_engine_hud_update_player called via inline asm with ECX=player_handle, EAX=-1, EBX=0 matching the exact register state observed at call sites in disassembly.
uncertain: The MOVSX/AND 0x80000001 sign-extension idiom in the networked team-assignment path exactly matches the original MSVC codegen but is unusual; verify against disassembly if team colors break in networked play.

inferred: Offset 0x24 in the player control slot is a weapon interaction index, reset to NONE when aim assist is cleared.
The name 'seat_index' for param_2 may actually refer to a weapon index since slot+0x20 is used as desired_weapon_index in player_control_get_facing.

0xb8e40.name: update_client_start

comments.summary: Resets client action-queue data and reserves one queue slot per active player handle. Asserts update_client_globals.initialized and queue_index!=NONE.

inferred: update_client_globals struct begins at 0x45b1d0: byte initialized at +0x00, data_t* queue at +0x90 (0x45b260). Struct size ~0x10494 per FUN_000b8d70 csmemset arg.
uncertain: Globals 0x45b1d0 (initialized byte) and 0x45b260 (queue data_t*) are not yet declared in kb.json; function uses hardcoded addresses. If/when player_queues_new has more functions ported, consider formalising update_client_globals in types.h.

comments.summary: Retrieve next server update snapshot for a machine; copies 0x204 bytes from server buffer

inferred: The call to system_milliseconds at function entry is a timing checkpoint with discarded return value.
Queue datum layout: +0x04=new_action_flags, +0x08=prev_action_flags (persistent mask), +0x0c..+0x24=action fields (facing, throttle, trigger, weapon/grenade/zoom).
The 0x4D0 mask (bits 4,6,7,10) applied to prev_action_flags likely represents sticky/persistent button flags (e.g. binoculars, zoom, alt_attack).
uncertain: The re-read of action_count from memory inside loop 1 (rather than using the local) may be an artifact of the original compiler or may be intentional for volatile-like behavior.

inferred: The action buffer at 0x45b264 is organized as 128 circular slots of 0x208 bytes each, with a uint16_t action count at slot offset +4.
The valid action count range [1, 16] likely corresponds to the maximum number of players (16 slots allocated in update_client_new).

comments.summary: Apply player actions from network machine into server queue; validates facing floats

inferred: The REP MOVSD copies exactly 8 dwords (0x20 bytes) matching the player action structure size.

comments.summary: Create server-side update snapshot from current queue state; pushes to client buffer

inferred: The internal helpers at 0xb9040 and 0xb97b0 likely belong to player_queues_new.obj but are not yet in kb.json.
The first assert checks game_connection()==0 which corresponds to _game_connection_local enum value.
The second assert string references both action_collection and update_client_globals.initialized but only the initialized flag at 0x45b1d0 is actually tested.
The ticks parameter is passed by address to update_server_get_update as update_number, which may modify it during the loop.

0xba710.name: any_player_is_in_the_air
inferred: Unit offset 0x64 is likely an animation or movement state enum (0=ground, 1=airborne/jumping).
Vehicle tag offset 0x17C bit 0x40 likely indicates the vehicle type supports airborne detection (e.g. flying vehicles).
uncertain: Object+0x428 byte meaning is unclear; compared > 2 as altitude or airtime threshold.

comments.summary: Clears a combined PVS bitvector, OR-folds in per-player/root-cluster visibility rows, and merges the current parent-object cluster. Live GDB during the co-op respawn regression showed the C body returning cleanly; the crash was in the reverse-thunk handoff, not this function logic.

inferred: Despite the kb.json name 'player_set_respawn_timer', the binary assert string says 'powerup_type>=0 && powerup_type<NUMBER_OF_PLAYER_POWERUPS' — this function manages powerup countdown timers (active camo, full spectrum vision) stored at player+0x68 as an int16_t array indexed by powerup_type.
uncertain: The unit fields at +0x1b4 (flag 0x10) and +0x3d2 (powerup type written on timer slot first-fill) are not yet named in types.h; they belong to some unit_data_t subtype retrieved via object_get_and_verify_type with type_mask=3.

inferred: The equipment_handle parameter (kb.json says vehicle_handle) actually refers to an equipment/powerup object, not a vehicle, based on type mask 8 and eqip tag group.
Tag offset 0x308 is the powerup type enum: 1=double_speed, 2=overshield, 3=active_camo, 4=full_spectrum_vision, 5=health.
Tag offset 0x30c is the duration in seconds as a float, multiplied by 30 to get game ticks.
uncertain: Functions 0xbaf90, 0xbb040, 0xbb0f0 are player notification helpers taking ESI as player_handle; their exact purpose (sound, visual effect, HUD message) is not confirmed.

comments.summary: Pre-tick player update. Originally reverted in ea68d9e due to ABI bugs; re-ported cleanly in 85d2748 after full disassembly-level audit. Split-screen multiplayer (spawn/move/look/shoot) verified in xemu.

Post-tick player update: ticks double-speed countdown, handles telefrag effect/kill, scans BSP-switch trigger volumes, advances BSP nibble counter, SP all-dead restart. Re-ported after pre-audit verified all 9 direct-address cast calls (0xa2ed0, 0xa2930, 0x1a7f80, 0xbc4b0 EBX-reg, 0x13d7f0, 0x18ef00, 0x100500, 0xbdb00 EBX-reg, 0x100380). Split-screen telefrag verified in xemu.

inferred: The magic value 0x5ccac at scenario+0x474 indicates the syntax data table was already set up for the current scenario.
Datum offset +6 bit 0x8 marks a syntax node as persistent (not deleted during dispose).
0x2f3708 is an array of pointers to hs_external_global structs whose first field (offset 0) is a char* name.
0x27d504 is hs_external_global_count as int16_t (observed value 0x1BB = 443 in the static image).
Scenario globals are at scenario_tag+0x4a8 as a tag_block with element size 0x5c, where offset 0 of each element is the name string.
file_read_into_buffer at 0x199540 takes (file_ref_t*, int* size_out) and returns allocated buffer pointer or NULL.
tag_block_add_element at 0x1b9ad0 takes (void* tag_block) and returns int16_t index or -1 on failure.
FUN_0xc4660 is hs_load_source_file and takes a file_reference via @EBX register arg.
uncertain: The csstrcmp at 0x8dcb0 name is inferred from its strcmp-like byte comparison behavior; could be csstrncmp or similar.

inferred: The backward newline counting loop uses MOVSX CX to int, treating the line counter as int16_t.
uncertain: The second condition checks nl != NULL (EAX from strchr), not error_text != NULL, which means the detailed format is only used when error_text actually contained a newline.

inferred: FUN_0xc4900 is hs_report_compile_error taking @ESI=error_text, @EBX=element_ptr, @EDI=source_start, plus 1 stack arg for error_info.

uncertain: The error argument order in the validation failure path may swap error_info and error_text relative to hs_console_evaluate; verified from disassembly push order.

inferred: The globals at 0x46b6e0-0x46b805 form the hs_compile_globals structure used by the script compiler.
0xc73a0 (hs_type_check_expression) takes EDI as an implicit register argument holding the syntax datum index.
The first call to hs_function_table_get (0xc3d00) at address 0xc7a5c has its return value discarded; the original binary calls it twice, once with the raw func_idx and once re-reading from node+2.
uncertain: When node[2] < 0 on a script-reference node, script_element is read uninitialized from the stack local at EBP-8; this appears to be original behavior.
The hs_parse_expression function at 0xc7be0 takes @EAX as a register arg pointing to the cursor variable; if this function modifies ESI internally the skip_whitespace call pattern may interact.
The hs_compile_source_setup function at 0xc5730 takes @EDI as source_file_size and one stack arg; the decompiler shows unaff_EDI confirming the register arg pattern.

inferred: caa80 (hs_get_thread_script_name) takes ESI=thread_index as register arg, returns script name string.
cd840 (hs_execute_thread) takes EAX=thread_index as register arg.
cc1d0 (hs_default_value) takes EAX=thread_index as register arg plus two stack args (type, dest).
cb230 (hs_global_value_store) takes EDI=loop_var as register arg, only active when bit 15 set.
Return type changed from void to int based on disassembly showing EAX set on all exit paths; callers at 0xc5212 and 0xc4b2f ignore the return value.
uncertain: The function at 0xcc1d0 (hs_default_value) takes @EAX plus 2 stack args; the exact semantics of the second stack arg (thread_ptr+0x14 vs thread_ptr+0x14 as dest) need verification.

inferred: Globals 0x5aa698 and 0x5aa694 are hs_object_list_header_data and hs_object_list_reference_data respectively, created by the function at 0xce150 with names 'object list header' and 'list object reference'.

comments.summary: If local_player_index is valid, forwards to hud_messaging_set_vehicle_notification with param_3=1 and param_4=-1. Called when player picks up equipment.
If local_player_index is valid, forwards to hud_messaging_set_vehicle_notification with seat_index and param_4=1. Called when player enters a vehicle seat.

inferred: Both hud_player_set_vehicle and hud_player_set_vehicle_seat have identical implementations that delegate to 0xd5240 with zeroed extra params.
Named hud_messaging_set_vehicle_notification based on callers hud_player_set_vehicle and hud_player_set_vehicle_seat; address falls within hud_messaging.obj range.
param_3 is a seat-related counter that accumulates into +0x88 of the message slot structure.
param_4 low byte stored at +0x8a is likely a notification type or seat index.
uncertain: The internal helper 0xd5070 uses ESI as a register arg for vehicle tag matching; not yet in kb.json to avoid thunk recursion.

inferred: Name ui_widget_is_main_menu_loaded for callee at 0xe43f0 inferred from behavior: checks if widget named the_main_menu is loaded.
Name ui_widget_get_attract_mode_flag for callee at 0xe46a0 inferred from behavior: returns a single byte flag at 0x46cc86.
Name ui_widget_stop_attract_mode for callee at 0xe4640 inferred from behavior: stops attract music via dsound tag lookup and clears the attract flag.
Name event_manager_get_last_event_time inferred from behavior: returns dword at 0x46bd44 which is written by event_manager_dispatch on non-empty events.
The interface globals block element at offset 0x140 contains 16 tag_reference entries (0x10 bytes each); the tag_index is at offset 0xc within each reference.
NUMBER_OF_INTERFACE_TAGS is 16, matching the CMP SI,0x10 in the assert guard.
The color_table tag group is 'colo' (0x636f6c6f) with 0x30-byte elements; ARGB float color is at offset 0x20 within each element.
Parameter 5 (color_tag_index) is an interface tag index for color lookup, not alpha; parameter 6 (color_index) is a short color table index, not tab_stop_count.
The 16-byte local buffer is a 4-float ARGB color filled by interface_get_color and passed to draw_string_set_font.
EDI register arg carries the line_handle; confirmed from PUSH EDI before CALL 0x119320 at 0xe3416 and PUSH EDI before CALL 0x1196d0 at 0xe346e/0xe3483.
0x46c418 is a byte activity flag set to 1 on each keypress and cleared to 0 by SETZ when the 30-tick timeout fires and the flag was non-zero.
The keystroke at [state+2+count*4] is a raw 4-byte dword matching the buffered_key_read entries at 0x46bc0c; struct layout is unconfirmed beyond dword size.
Age counter is at line+0x120 (int); lines are evicted after age exceeds 0x96 (150 ticks), confirmed from CMP ECX,0x96 / JLE in disasm.
terminal_remove_line at 0xe3410 takes the line handle via EDI register argument; terminal_get_line sets EDI=[0x46c410] before calling it to evict the tail entry.
The doubly-linked list uses offsets +0x4 (prev handle) and +0x8 (next handle) within the datum; [0x46c40c] is head and [0x46c410] is tail.
terminal_update's return in AL (from terminal_process_input) is ignored by its sole caller main_loop; the void return type in kb.json is correct.
console_is_active at 0xff4c0 returns the byte at [0x46cf60], which is the console-open flag; when true, line aging (terminal_age_lines) is skipped.
stack_memory_pool_realloc at 0x11fbe0 is the name inferred from the stack_memory_pool.c source path in its assert strings and its role as the realloc counterpart to stack_memory_pool_deallocate.
stack_memory_pool_realloc at 0x11fbe0 is the name inferred from the stack_memory_pool.c source path in its assert strings and its role as the realloc counterpart to stack_memory_pool_deallocate.

comments.summary: Checks whether a deferred UI error should be shown once the main menu is ready, then routes through the existing error display path.

inferred: The function at 0x1006c0 checks whether a game map is loaded by testing global 0x46da34 != 0.
The function at 0x1c8510 is sound_looping_start in game_sound.c; its third arg is a float (1.0f) passed as dword 0x3f800000 on the stack.
The widget struct field at offset +0x28 is named 'previous' (prev_sibling) based on the assert string 'child->previous == NULL'.

comments.summary: Depth-first search over a widget subtree for the first node whose tag handle (+0x0) equals tag_handle. Checks current node, then recurses into each child (+0x34) following next_sibling (+0x2c).
Applies widget focus by retargeting to a focusable candidate when needed, then rewriting parent focused-descendant links.

inferred: Widget +0x38 behaves as the focused-descendant link, and +0x12 gates whether the requested widget is focusable.
ui_widget_close_children at 0xe4890 takes widget ptr in EAX and recursively closes all first_child chain nodes.

Widget struct offsets: +0x00 tag_index, +0x08 local_player_index (word), +0x0e widget_type (word), +0x13 pauses_game (byte), +0x14 being_deleted (byte), +0x28 prev_sibling, +0x2c next_sibling, +0x30 parent, +0x34 first_child, +0x3c text_data, +0x40 list_items, +0x48 list_header_widget, +0x4c list_skin_data.
The 4-element arrays at 0x46cc20 and 0x46cc30 represent widget stack roots and widget list heads respectively, indexed by stack number (0-3).
Widget nodes are linked through offset +0xc, which appears to be a next-sibling or next-in-list pointer within each widget structure.
uncertain: The relationship between the root widget at 0x46cc20[i] and the list at 0x46cc30[i] is unclear; the root is closed via ui_widget_close while the list nodes are only deallocated from the pool without closure.

comments.summary: Walks up the parent chain (+0x30) from widget to root, then searches the widget tree for tag_handle. If found, tail-calls ui_widget_apply_focus; otherwise logs an error.

inferred: thread_new at 0x81630 prototype inferred from push count and CreateThread wrapper pattern in thread_win32.obj.
0x46cc80 is a 16-bit field (word ptr in disasm) not 32-bit as Ghidra decompiler suggests.
0xe5590 is a stdcall thread proc (RET 4) used as filesystem check callback.
The pointer at 0x31e04c points to a widget system state structure; field at +4 is a debug_malloc'd memory pool (0x4000 bytes), field at +8 is the pool size.
The 0x68-byte block at 0x46cc20 is static UI widget state cleared on both init and dispose.
The global at 0x5aa45c stores the current rendering player index as uint16_t, clamped so -1 maps to 0.
The 0xe73c0 callee is a recursive widget renderer taking (widget, bounds, offset_pair, int, int) with 5 stack args.
uncertain: The float constants at 0x2533c0 (0.0), 0x2533c8 (1.0), 0x255ed4 (0.95), and 0x2602c8 (255.0) are read-only data section floats whose symbolic names are unknown.
The 0x1b9930 callee is a tag lookup function (tag_group, name, ...) but its full prototype beyond 2 args is not confirmed.
FUN_000e5380/FUN_000e53e0/FUN_000e5440/FUN_000e68e0 are called through register-sensitive wrappers; exact semantic names remain unresolved.
FUN_00100620 at 0x100620 is only confirmed as a no-args helper called when all root widgets are gone.

inferred: The __stricmp call at 0x1dd801 is an MSVC CRT function; accessed via hardcoded address since no CRT object is tracked.
The virtual keyboard init at 0xf53a0 likely belongs to a virtual_keyboard.obj but no such object is yet in kb.json.
FUN_00100000 at 0x100000 clears byte_46DA25; semantic name remains uncertain.
ui_widget_load_by_name_or_tag at 0xe84e0 takes 7 cdecl args; return value is a widget pointer.
main_halt_entry at 0x102c40 is __noreturn infinite loop calling subsystem ticks (input, rendering, presentation).

uncertain: FUN_000ab720 at 0xab720 is only known as a bool gate used in multiplayer pause handling; semantic name is unresolved.
FUN_00124c40 and FUN_001257a0 (network_client_manager.c asserts) are called for side-effect parity; their higher-level intent in pause flow is still uncertain.

inferred: 0xf5640 and 0xf6740 appear to be bink-video screen update functions but their exact .obj is uncertain.
The pending_load struct fields at offsets +8 and +10 are read as dword by MSVC but typed as int16_t; the upper bytes are garbage but ignored by callees.
Inlined grid dimension helper (originally at 0x1008a0 with EBX register arg) computes smallest (h,v) grid where h*v >= num_players.
Extra-wide flag gives player 0 a double-width column when grid has spare slots.
uncertain: Field naming for viewport_bounds_t y0/x0/y1/x1 ordering assumed correct from types.h; disassembly accesses match.

inferred: Flag at 0x46da3f is game_state_load_core_pending based on kb.json naming of 0x46da3e.
The 0x1bfee0 call is cache_files_precache, triggered when byte 0x46da54 is set.
Tail-calls input_tick (0xcf7e0) rather than returning directly; C version uses normal call which adds a RET instruction.
uncertain: Ring buffer modulus is 15 (0xf), not 16; confirmed from IDIV ECX where ECX=0xf.

0x101d90.name: main_save_current_solo_map
inferred: 0x1db2b3 is fwrite, despite Ghidra labelling it FID_conflict:_fread; call context (write mode open, file pointer, fixed-size struct) confirms write semantics.

0x101f00.name: main_load_ui_scenario
inferred: 0x86cb0 is a camera or director subsystem helper, inferred from the constant argument it receives and its position relative to cinematic calls.
0x85180 is a cinematic-state initializer, inferred from call order and constant zero argument.
0xe43d0 is the UI widget flag-2 setter, inferred from its single boolean-like argument and call site immediately after widget lookup.
uncertain: Exact semantics of 0x86cb0 beyond its observed constant argument are unknown; it may reset or prime a camera track rather than simply disposing state.
Exact semantics of 0x85180 beyond its observed constant argument are unknown; could be cinematic-state reset or first-frame init.

inferred: Global pointers at 0x31fc1c/0x31fc3c/0x31fc44 are default camera position/forward/up vectors.
0xff4c0 is likely game_in_editor predicate; 0x86410 returns object type.
uncertain: The REP MOVSD copy from window+0x58 to window+0x04 may be a previous-frame camera snapshot; exact semantic not confirmed.

inferred: 0x7e0b0 is bitmap_2d_new; creates a 2D bitmap from width/height/unk/depth.
uncertain: Tile coordinate layout in the int16_t[4] array inferred from PUSH order; inner_col/inner_row/outer_col/outer_row mapping may need verification.

inferred: Rotation inverse assumes orthogonal matrix (transpose equals inverse); this is standard for rigid-body transforms in game engines.

Original x87 codegen stores intermediate products to out[2], out[3], out[6] before adding/subtracting sine*axis components via FADD/FSUB [EAX+N]; the C version collapses each pair into a single store of the final value which is behaviorally identical but not instruction-for-instruction identical.

comments.summary: Builds a 4x3 matrix from basis_data via FUN_001093b0, then overwrites translation with the provided position vector.

inferred: Scale field at offset 0x00 is compared as integer 0x3f800000 (1.0f) rather than as float, matching the original binary's integer CMP instruction.
Matrix layout: 4-byte scale at offset 0, followed by 3x3 float rotation at offsets 0x04-0x24.
The multiplication order is out = b * a for the rotation part, with translation incorporating a's scale factor.
uncertain: The original uses SSE MULPS/ADDPS for the 3x3 and translation parts; the scalar x87 reimplementation may produce slightly different rounding in edge cases.

inferred: Matrix rows: scale, forward, left (forward x up cross product), up, zero translation.
Name random_math_get_local_seed_address inferred: returns pointer to static seed at 0x46e3f8, used with random_range.
The function finds the component with the smallest absolute value and produces a perpendicular vector by zeroing that component and swapping the other two with one negated.
angles[0]=yaw, angles[1]=pitch; standard spherical-to-cartesian conversion.
Pool header layout inferred from field accesses: +0x00 tag/name_ptr, +0x04 base_address, +0x08 pool_size, +0x0c slot_count; slot table at +0x34 (slot_count * 4 bytes).
table[0] is seeded with a self-pointer via csmemcpy to initialize the free-slot list.

comments.summary: Returns block size flags masked to low 31 bits after asserting non-null block header.

inferred: The high bit of block_hdr[0] is treated as an in-use flag and cleared by masking with 0x7fffffff.
The usable_size == 0x20 check (LEA EDX,[EAX-0x20]; TEST EDX,EDX; JA) rejects blocks with no payload beyond the 0x20-byte header overhead.
The call to 0x11eb10 (block-slot-index getter taking ESI) is inlined as blk[1] since the block is known non-NULL at that point.
Returns block_hdr+0x1c in EAX (LEA EAX,[ESI+0x1c]) but kb.json declares void return; callers do not use the return value.
Internal callees 0x11ef50 (valid_block: EAX=block_hdr,ECX=pool) and 0x11efd0 (unlink_block: ESI=block_hdr,EDI=pool) are called via inline asm due to non-standard register conventions.
alloc_count incremented only when old_size==0 (new alloc, not resize); uses SETLE semantics: TEST EBX,EBX; SETLE DL; ADD ECX,EDX.
uncertain: Internal callee 0x11f750 (alloc_or_resize) register convention: EAX=new_size, ECX=pool, stack=[block_hdr, file, line]; confirmed from disassembly of call site but callee internals not fully analyzed.

comments.summary: Seat-enter matrix bridge: decodes one animation node into an 0x800-byte stack buffer via FUN_00121d60, then converts the decoded basis+position slice into an output matrix via FUN_001094d0.

inferred: Function name 'terminal_string_process_tabs' is the binary name; despite being in draw_string.obj, its sole purpose is to forward rendered text out over the telnet debug console (send_endpoint at 0x82f50).
The CRLF prefix string is a literal two-byte '\r\n' at 0x261f2c (not a null-terminated string); the hard-coded length of 2 is confirmed from 'PUSH 0x2' in disassembly.
Function is from object_lights.c based on assert path, but grouped in objects.obj.
The light data table lives at 0x5a90bc; the cluster partition at 0x5a90b0.
Function is from object_lights.c based on assert path, but grouped in objects.obj.
Float constants at 0x2568bc (pi/2) and 0x254a58 (pi/4) serve as angle thresholds for the light position offset computation.
uncertain: The marker buffer size is inferred as 0x6c bytes from the highest offset read (0x68); the actual struct layout is unknown.
Tag offsets 0x14 (falloff_angle), 0x18 (cutoff), 0x20 (forward_modifier), 0x24 (gel_modifier), 0x28 (tertiary_modifier) are named by function, not confirmed by tag definitions.

inferred: datum_absolute_index_to_index at 0x119270 is functionally a datum_try_and_get; returns datum pointer as int, or 0 on failure.
Returns object_data_t* from entry->object (header+0x8); constructs datum handle as (entry->unk_0 salt << 16) | slot_index and stores in iter->last_handle.
Bit 0x10000 in object flags is _object_garbage_bit; bit 0x20000 is likely _object_being_deleted_bit based on the 0x30000 mask guard.
object_data_t+0xC0 (unk_192) is the garbage linked list next-pointer field.
cluster_partition_remove_object at 0x1919a0 takes (partition_ptr, object_handle, first_cluster_ref_ptr) based on push order and usage pattern in callers.
0x13e510 is an object-child-list unlink helper taking EAX=ptr-to-first-child-ref and EBX=object_handle as register args with zero stack args.
uncertain: The re-fetch of header at 0x13fdc8 after the partition-remove call may be needed because the partition-remove could invalidate the cached header pointer, but this is not confirmed.

inferred: Model tag node count is stored at offset 0xb8 in the 'mode' tag data.
Object tag definition field at offset 0x34 is the model tag index; value -1 means no model.

flag=0 marks the object as garbage (sets obj->flags bit 0, clears datum.unk_2 bit 1); flag!=0 unmarks it. Child propagation via FUN_0013ee60 (0x13ee60) is gated on the tag having a children block (tag+0x34 != -1) and bit 0 of obj->flags being in the wrong state for the requested transition.
unk_408 and unk_412 at offsets 0x198 and 0x19c are interpolation buffer references used by object_header_block_reference_get.
Model tag offset 0xb8 is the region count as int16_t.
FUN_0013ee60 propagates deletion state to child objects attached via the tag children block at tag+0x34.
FUN_0013eff0 removes the object from an indexed name list using object+0x6A as the slot index and the array at 0x46f07c.
uncertain: The weapon_is_flag check may also apply to oddball or skull objects, not just CTF flags.

inferred: Thin wrapper calling FUN_00140bc0 (0x140bc0) with param_2=0; param_2=1 would also delete sibling objects recursively. FUN_00140bc0 is the real implementation: sets datum.unk_2 bit 3 (0x08, pending removal), clears the active bit in obj->flags, and calls FUN_0013eff0 (0x13eff0, EDI=object_handle) to clear the object's name-list entry.
The field at object+0x1A0 (unk_416) is a block reference to the object node matrix array, where each node matrix is 0x34 (52) bytes.
Function 0x124730 is likely model_find_markers or animation_find_markers, taking 9 cdecl args including animation graph data, marker name, object nodes, node matrices, and flags.
Function 0x1090e0 initializes a 52-byte identity-like transform (scale=1.0 + identity 3x3 rotation + zero translation), corresponding to the marker local transform.
uncertain: The flags expression (obj->flags >> 12) & 0xffffff01 produces a value where bit 0 indicates mirroring and upper bits may encode additional node/marker flags, but the exact semantics of the upper bits are unclear.

inferred: The child_marker parameter contains a matrix4x3 at offset 0x38 which represents the marker's local-to-world transform.
The cross-product re-orthogonalization computes left=cross(forward,up) then up_new=cross(left,forward) to ensure orthogonality.
The byte at obj+0xD0 is a parent node index, not part of the float array unk_208[5] that the struct declares at that offset — the struct layout at 0xD0 may need revision.
FUN_0010a110 builds a 4x3 matrix from forward, up, position; FUN_00140eb0 is object_get_node_matrix; FUN_00109850 is 4x3 matrix multiply.

The assert_valid_real_matrix4x3 macro expands to ~30 lines of inline validation checking scale, forward/left/up magnitude, perpendicularity dot products, and position for NaN/Inf; repeated 3 times at lines 0xb37, 0xb69, 0xb77.
Parent node matrix scale adjustment (when scale != 1.0) copies the parent matrix to a local buffer and resets scale to 1.0, then scales the orientation position by the original scale factor.
uncertain: The 0xfe0 type mask (bits 5-11) is labeled _object_mask_cannot_interpolate based on the assertion string; exact bit-to-type mapping not fully verified.

inferred: Bit 11 of object_data_t.flags indicates the object is connected to the map, based on the disconnect/reconnect pattern around the flag test.
The byte at object_data_t offset 0xD0 stores the parent_node_index as a uint8_t, overlapping with the first byte of the unk_208 float array.
unk_200 at object_data+0xC8 is the first child object handle in a parent-child chain.
FUN_00141b70 is object_compute_node_matrices, taking a single int object_handle and returning void.
Marker result struct is 0x6C (108) bytes; first dword is node index, 4x3 matrix at offset +4.
Parent marker offsets for position/up/forward within the 0x6C struct are 0x60, 0x3C, 0x54 respectively.
In the else branch, the destination matrix for object_compute_child_marker_position aliases parent_markers+0x38.
In the else branch, the destination matrix for object_compute_child_marker_position aliases parent_markers+0x38.
decals_update_for_new_map (0x98e70) name is inferred from its behavior: it iterates all decal data, clears locked/permanent flags, and resets the global lock/permanent counts.

comments.summary: Validates decal cache index and local_vertex_cache handle, then removes the corresponding LRUV block via FUN_0011d8f0.

inferred: The 12-byte vertex buffer struct layout (+0x0 D3D resource header=1, +0x4 GPU memory pointer from game_state_gpu_alloc, +0x8 lock flags=0) is derived from disassembly offsets MOV [EAX],1 / LEA ESI,[EAX+4] / MOV [EAX+8],0 and matches a minimal D3D resource header.
game_state_gpu_alloc (0x1bfd00) name is inferred from context: it allocates from the game-state GPU region and logs to gamestate.txt; the second arg is always 0 at this call site.
uncertain: The lruv_cache entry_size arg 0x800 and capacity arg 0xa00 are confirmed from disassembly pushes but their semantic meaning (bytes per entry vs. entry count) is not independently verified.

inferred: D3DResource_Release (0x1ed930) is stdcall with RET 0x4, confirmed from disassembly; the call site uses POP ECX after the call which is inconsistent (the RET 0x4 already cleans the arg).

comments.summary: Thin cdecl trampoline for decal teardown: forwards decal_index directly to 0x15b530.

inferred: 0x31fc1c is a pointer to a global forward/identity vector used for plane distance computation in frustum building.

The frustum output is a packed float array with a well-defined layout: [0..3] bounds, [4..0x10] world_to_view, [0x11..0x1d] view_to_world, [0x1e..0x35] 6 planes, [0x36..0x37] z values, [0x38..0x49] corners/positions, [0x4a..0x4f] AABB, [0x50..0x62] projection.
uncertain: The FPTAN instruction behavior and FPU stack management in the inv_tan_x/inv_tan_y computation may not be perfectly reproduced by the C version since the compiler may use different FPU instruction sequences.

comments.summary: Scales byte-space detail-object coordinates into world units: (param_2 * *(float*)0x261518 + param_1) * *(float*)0x253f78.
Allocates 0xa430 bytes via game_state_malloc for structure detail objects, stores pointer at 0x4d8ea0, and inits 4 fields at +0xa420..+0xa42c.
Stores param_1 to 0x4d8eac, sets flag byte 0x4d8ea4, and accumulates delta into 0x4d8ea8 as (param_1 - old_value).
4-component dot product helper: returns sum(param_1[i] * param_2[i]) for i=0..3.
Asserts 0x4d8ea0 is non-null, zeroes the 0xa430-byte detail object block, and clears byte at +0x520e.
Allocates structure runtime decals globals via game_state_malloc with group size 4, stores pointer at 0x4d8ec8. Asserts on failure.
Asserts 0x4d8ec8 is non-null, then zeroes the first byte of the decals globals block.
Empty function (RET only). No-op dispose for structure detail objects.
Empty function (RET only). No-op dispose for structure runtime decals.

Updates structure runtime decals per cluster visibility transition: deletes decals for clusters leaving visibility, emits decals for eligible clusters entering/currently visible, then clears the runtime decals update flag byte at 0x4d8ec8[0].
Sets both visibility toggle bytes at 0x505702 and 0x505701 to param_1.
Cluster marker begin: asserts marker is not already set, increments ref counter, and sets initialized flag.
Cluster marker query helper: validates marker state and cluster index bounds, then returns whether cluster_marker[cluster_index] differs from current marker id 0x4d92e4.
Cluster marker set helper: validates marker state and bounds, writes current marker id into cluster_marker[cluster_index] when not already marked, and returns whether a new mark occurred.

Portal/sphere visibility gate: performs signed-plane distance and portal-center radius tests, projects hit/portal vertices into portal plane space, then runs a 2D inclusion check with sqrt(radius^2 - plane_distance^2).

Recursive cluster flood helper for structure_find_in_cluster: records current cluster when space remains, marks it visited, walks connected portals, and recurses into unvisited adjacent clusters whose portal sphere test passes.

inferred: find_files_begin at 0x19a040 and find_files_next at 0x19aed0 are files_windows.obj helpers for directory enumeration.

file_read at 0x19ab50 identified by error string 'file_read' in its decompilation.

The 6th argument to path_split (flags & 1 from ref->unk_4[0]) controls whether paths are treated as having a trailing directory component.
file_ref_t.unk_4 is a 16-bit flags field where bits 0-3 are name flags (directory, parent_directory, filename, extension).

uncertain: The file_error function at 0x19a450 takes file_ref in EAX as a register arg; if a future port adds it to kb.json with @eax, the inline asm call should be replaced.

inferred: Assert string references tag_files.c not tag_groups.c, suggesting this function was originally defined in tag_files.c but compiled into tag_groups.obj.

Field order in the color struct is alpha at [+0], red at [+4], green at [+8], blue at [+0xc] — inferred from assertion strings naming alpha/red/green/blue in sequence and confirmed by ESI offsets in disassembly.

uncertain: The upper bound check for style uses 'CMP DI,0x4; JL ok' meaning style < 4, implying NUMBER_OF_TEXT_STYLES=4 (styles 0..3); but the boundary value of 4 itself is rejected, consistent with an exclusive upper bound.

inferred: tag_get return value is discarded; the call is used solely for its assertion/crash side-effect on invalid tag indices — confirmed by absence of any use of EAX after the call at 0x19b8bd.
OR byte ptr [EAX+0xb6],0x20 confirmed from disassembly — sets bit 5 of byte at object_data_t+0xb6, one byte before unk_183 (0xb7) used by unit_delete.
Global at 0x4e4cf8 is a pointer to a 5-byte struct {int16_t, int16_t, uint8_t} used for units per-tick update state tracking.

comments.summary: Resolves seat animation mode for target seat label, computes enter-position matrix and marker positions via animation/marker helpers, and writes up to three output vectors (out_pos_a/out_pos_b/out_pos_c). Returns 1 on success, 0 when no valid mode/path is found.

inferred: The unk_456 field (offset 0x1C8) in unit_data_t guards the allegiance check, likely representing a rider or owner handle.
The return semantics are inverted: returns true when NO matching seat occupant or friendly child was found, meaning the seat is available.

comments.summary: Resolves a unit handle via object_get_and_verify_type(mask 3) and returns bit 6 of unit_data_t.unk_436 as the alive flag.

inferred: The byte at offset 0x253 (unk_595) likely represents an AI/unit behavior state enum; values 0x21, 0x19, 0x18 correspond to combat-active states.
The byte at offset 0x23D (unk_573) value 3 likely represents a passive/non-threatening sub-state that suppresses the dangerous classification.
Bit 2 of offset 0x248 (unk_584) acts as a suppression flag that prevents units in state 0x18/0x19 from being considered dangerous.
Seat flag bit 2 (0x4) designates driver/rider occupant slot at unk_724, bit 3 (0x8) designates gunner occupant slot at unk_728.

comments.summary: Returns the equipment datum handle stored in the unit's equipment slot (offset 0x2C8).
Attempts to add a grenade (powerup_type 6) to the unit's inventory. Increments grenade count at unit+0x2CE, plays effect for local players, and deletes the equipment object.

inferred: Names object_disconnect_from_map (0x13fd00), object_set_garbage (0x13ffc0), object_delete (0x140cc0), item_activate_equipment_effect (0xf67b0), and item_attach_to_unit (0xf69c0) are inferred from context and decompilation, not from assert strings.
Callee 0x140cc0 is object_delete (wrapper calling 0x140bc0 with param_2=0, i.e. no sibling recursion).
The second inner check (i == unit->unk_674) is dead code because the outer condition already excludes i == unk_674; preserved for binary fidelity.
Function is a simple linear scan of the 4-slot weapon array at unit+0x2A8 for the first NONE slot.
Function name unit_set_animation inferred from behavior: sets animation graph tag index, animation index, and frame counter on a unit object.

The name tag_get_name for 0x1ba1f0 is inferred from behavior: loads tag_index into EDI, calls tag lookup via 0x1b9bf0 (EDI register arg), and returns the name pointer at offset 0x10 of the tag header.
uncertain: The debug globals at 0x5054fc (debug animation logging flag) and 0x5ac9f8 (debug unit filter handle) are accessed as hardcoded addresses; their struct membership and exact semantics are not fully confirmed.

inferred: The random throw direction uses the unit's unk_492 (offset 0x1EC) as the forward vector, with angle ~pi/8 and scale ~0.027-0.04.
The weapon remembers the detaching unit via weapon+0x1B0 write.
uncertain: The exact name of 0x1a9240 (unit_set_seat_state) is uncertain; it receives the unit handle and the root position vector.

inferred: The mode element at antr_tag+0xc contains animation modes; each mode has sub-animations at +0x58 with weapon labels at sub_anim+0xB0.
uncertain: The multi-weapon flag logic checks key_data pointers at mode+0x44 offsets +4, +6, +8 for non-NONE int16_t values; exact semantics of these weapon animation channels are unconfirmed.

comments.summary: Finds the best seat for a unit to enter another unit. Iterates seats, checks distances and animation compatibility. Returns seat state (0=none, 1=approach, 2=enter) and writes seat index.
Checks if the unit's current weapon is in state 2 or 3 (offset 0x211). Returns true if busy, false if no weapon or in another state.

inferred: Returns int (pointer as int) for ABI compatibility with callers that pass the result to unit_try_animation_state.
Base seat label table at 0x32e484 contains 6 string pointers: asleep, empty, stand, empty, empty, flaming.
The function at 0xa8b30 dispatches through current_game_engine vtable offset 0x58 and is used for both weapon pickup and seat entry validation.
The function at 0xfae80 returns a char* label from the weapon tag definition at offset 0x30c, returning a default string at 0x25386f when the handle is NONE.

comments.summary: Determines whether the unit should swap a weapon. Returns true if the new weapon type is not held, or if held in current seat with equal/more ammo and current ammo above threshold.

inferred: 0x1ae290 is a register-arg (EAX) function that retrieves a unit animation tag pointer, used for weapon compatibility checks.
0x1acd70 is a register-arg (EAX) function with 3 stack args that checks whether a unit can use a given weapon based on animation and weapon tag data.
uncertain: The unk_696 array at offset 0x2B8 appears to hold weapon priority/ordering values used to select the best weapon when direction==0, but the exact semantics are unconfirmed.

inferred: FUN_001ae490 computes next weapon index; FUN_000fd360 attempts weapon placement; FUN_000de360 fires a unit event; FUN_001ab990 is a weapon-stow notification; FUN_000faf50 checks if weapon should be kept alive.
Validates 6 direction vectors via valid_real_normal3d and checks forward/up perpendicularity via valid_real_normal3d_perpendicular at 0x84a70.
The function at 0x1af620 is unit_verify_vectors, taking unit_handle in EAX and returning bool; it validates 6 direction vectors via assert_valid_real_normal3d.
The function at 0x49ac0 builds a textual location string for a unit given actor_index, unit_handle, a bool flag, buffer, and size (5 cdecl args).
uncertain: The exact prototype of 0x49ac0 is uncertain; arg3 appears to be a bool (PUSH 1) but could be an int flag.

inferred: FUN_0x21fb0 is assert_valid_real_normal3d (validates unit vector). FUN_0x1af6b0 is a profiling/trace function accepting a string label.
Weapon tag offset 0x4bc is a sound tag index for zoom-deactivation sound.
Player datum offset +2 is checked against -1 to verify player validity before playing sound.
The weapon attachment markers come from the animation sub-element at mode+0x58: parent marker at sub_anim+0x40, weapon marker at sub_anim+0x20.
When weapon readiness transitions to a new weapon, unk_696[weapon_index] records game_time_get() as a timestamp.
Marker struct layout inferred: rotation matrix at offset 0x38 and position at offset 0x60 within the marker output buffer.
Function names for callees are conservative: object_set_region_count (0x140160), object_adjust_interpolation_position (0x140230), etc. are best guesses based on behavior.
uncertain: 0x1ab7c0 uses three register args (EAX, EDI, BX); current inline asm saves/restores EDI and EBX via push/pop which may cause stack misalignment issues under some calling conventions.

comments.summary: Resolve tag handle to base/data pointer; asserts on group mismatch or null data

inferred: Offset +0x10 in the tag instance record is the tag name string pointer, accessed as entry[4] (dword at 0x10).
Global at 0x4e9250 is a pointer to an array of 0x20-byte cache request structures; field at offset 0x1c is an enable/active flag byte.
MAXIMUM_SIMULTANEOUS_CACHE_REQUESTS is 0x200 (512), derived from the CMP SI,0x200 bounds check.

GPU allocations grow downward from base_address: return = 0x345000 + (base_address - new_gpu_allocation_size). The GPU region ceiling is 0x40000 bytes. Log file is opened in 'w' mode on first call to 'd:\\gamestate.txt'.
uncertain: The second argument 'a2' is declared int in kb.json but is used as a %s format specifier in the log fprintf call, suggesting it is actually a const char* group/category name string, analogous to the group_name parameter in game_state_malloc.

inferred: Checks both bink handle (0x4ead60) and initialized flag (0x4ead58) to determine if bink playback is active.
Only checks bink handle (0x4ead60) for non-zero; does not require subsystem initialized.
The function uses a custom register calling convention: alignment in EAX and alloc_size in ECX, with a plain RET (no stack cleanup).
Globals 0x4eae2c (memory_pool_size) and 0x4eae24 (memory_pool_base) are part of a bink_globals structure starting around 0x4ead58.
0xdc240 is a simple byte setter at 0x46bd41 (event suppression toggle). 0x231220 is BinkClose. 0x1c61d0 releases texture cache memory for bink.
The function at 0x1c5990 (bink memory pool allocator) uses two register args: @eax for alignment and @ecx for size, called via inline asm.
The D3D texture setup at 0x4ead40-0x4ead78 configures an NV2A linear X8R8G8B8 texture resource for the bink frame buffer.
uncertain: The bitmap header at 0x4ead10 uses format 10 (a8r8g8b8) but this format index mapping is inferred from the bits-per-pixel call and may not match the engine's enum exactly.

inferred: The 64-byte local buffer at EBP-0x40 is a sound source descriptor struct with fields: offset 0x00 int16_t spatialization_mode, offset 0x04 float scale, offset 0x08 float gain.
sound_start at 0x1ce180 belongs to sound_manager.obj based on assertion path string c:\halo\SOURCE\sound\sound_manager.c.
sound_class_get (0x1c89d0) takes its class index in SI register and is called via inline asm with +S constraint; return value in EAX is the float* record pointer.
uncertain: The float store offset is [record+0x0] and int16 store is [record+0x8], confirmed from disassembly; the record is 0xc bytes per class entry.

inferred: 0x1c88c0 is sound_get_class_definition, takes cdecl short class_index and returns pointer to a 0x2c-byte class definition struct at 0x32ed08 + index*0x2c.
Byte at offset 0x28 in the class definition struct is a suppress flag that prevents playback when set.
The promotion system uses a time accumulator at tag+0x88 and stores the last check timestamp at tag+0x8c, comparing against the global sound timestamp at 0x4eaf4c.
uncertain: The MOVSX SI,SI and IMUL pattern suggests promotion_count is sign-extended from short to int before the multiplication with interval.

inferred: 0x1ccbe0 takes EAX=channel_index and EDI=source_pointer as register args and returns distance squared in ST(0).
0x1c8310 is a cdecl function taking (int channel_index, void *source, float sqrt_distance) that evaluates channel suitability.
Local player listener slots are at 0x4eaf58 with stride 0x44, maximum 4 listeners.
uncertain: The assert for index bounds at line 0x430 appears unreachable given the loop bounds, suggesting it may be an inlined bounds-check macro.

inferred: Register-arg callees at 0x1cba20, 0x1cbb00, 0x1ccca0, 0x1cd5a0 are called via inline asm because they take arguments in EAX or EDI.
The constant at 0x2c1288 (8.9647f) is a time-to-distance conversion factor used to compute delayed sound start timing.
uncertain: The source struct layout is accessed by offsets (0x0=spatialization_mode, 0x4=scale, 0x18=forward vector) but the full struct type is unknown.

0x1cf730.name: sound_render
inferred: The constant at 0x25bc08 is 0.03f, used to convert millisecond deltas to sound-system time units.
The call to 0x1d9068 is MSVC __ftol2 (float-to-long truncation via ST0); replaced with a C cast in the lift.
Vtable calls at offsets +0x10 and +0x14 through the sound driver pointer at 0x4eaf48 are likely lock/unlock or begin-frame/end-frame pairs.
